title: albizia tanganyicensis subsp. tanganyicensis
intro: Albizia tanganyicensis subsp. tanganyicensis, also known as the East African mimosa, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its small, pink to white flowers and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The health benefits associated with Albizia tanganyicensis subsp. tanganyicensis include its potential to treat fever, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its use in promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
